hero.title: Online Image Splitter

aboutTool.title: What is AI Image Splitter?
aboutTool.descriptionMain: AI Image Splitter is a browser-based image splitting tool that divides one image into multiple equal parts. It includes aspect ratio presets like 3:4, 4:5, and 1:1, plus three split modes: Vertical, Horizontal, and Grid. Whether you are creating social media posts, preparing A4 print layouts, or building image collages, you can set everything up quickly and export right away. All processing happens locally on your device, and your images are never uploaded to a server. You can choose the aspect ratio and split mode, set rows and columns, adjust the selection box to define the split area, and export results as individual files or a ZIP in JPG, PNG, WebP, or BMP.

howItWorks.title: How to Use the Image Splitter
howItWorks.step1Description: Upload a JPG, PNG, GIF, or WebP image (up to 50 MB). Everything is processed in your browser, so your file never leaves your device.
howItWorks.step2Description: Choose an aspect ratio preset (Default, 4:5, 3:4, or 1:1) and a split mode (Vertical, Horizontal, or Grid), then adjust the selection box to define the split area.
howItWorks.step3Description: Preview the split result, then download individual images or export everything as a ZIP file. Output formats include JPG, PNG, WebP, and BMP.

footer.brandNameNote: Note: "AI" is part of our brand name. This tool does not use AI models, and all image processing runs locally in your browser.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Answers to common questions about supported formats, privacy, download options, and split settings.

whatIsImageSplitter.question: What can AI Image Splitter do?
whatIsImageSplitter.answer: It can split one image into multiple equal parts and lets you preview results before downloading. You can upload JPG, PNG, GIF, or WebP images, choose your split settings, and export individual files or a ZIP package.

howToSplitImage.question: How do I split an image step by step?
howToSplitImage.answer: After uploading your image, choose an aspect ratio and split mode, then set rows and columns and adjust the selection box for the split area. Once the preview looks right, download individual results or a ZIP package.

instagramGridMaker.question: Can I use it for Instagram grids and carousel posts?
instagramGridMaker.answer: Yes. You can create split results for grid and carousel posts, then download them in order for publishing.

splitImage3x3.question: How do I split an image into 2, 3, or 4 parts, or into 3x3 or 2x3 grids?
splitImage3x3.answer: Choose a split mode, then set rows and columns to create 2, 3, 4, or more parts. You can also use layouts like 3x3, 2x3, and 3x1.

supportedFormats.question: What upload and export formats are supported?
supportedFormats.answer: Uploads support JPG, PNG, GIF, and WebP (up to 50 MB, with recommended dimensions up to 8192x8192). Exports support JPG, PNG, WebP, and BMP, with individual downloads or ZIP packages.

freeToUse.question: Is this tool really free?
freeToUse.answer: Yes. No sign-up, no watermarks, no hidden fees, and no usage limits, as long as your files stay within supported formats and size limits.

imageQuality.question: Will image quality drop after splitting?
imageQuality.answer: If the source image quality is good, split results usually stay clear. We recommend using a high-quality source image and checking the preview before exporting.

privacySecurity.question: Will my images be uploaded to a server?
privacySecurity.answer: No. Processing happens locally in your browser, and your images are not uploaded to a server.

mobileFriendly.question: Can I use it on a phone or tablet?
mobileFriendly.answer: Yes. The site is optimized for mobile devices, so you can upload, configure, and download from your phone or tablet.

batchProcessing.question: Can I use it for A4 printing or multi-page layouts?
batchProcessing.answer: Yes. You can split large images into multiple parts using rows and columns for A4 printing or multi-page layouts, and you can also use the results in PDF documents.

customDimensions.question: How do I split an image in a 4:5 ratio?
customDimensions.answer: Choose the 4:5 aspect ratio preset first, then select a split mode and set rows and columns. After confirming the preview, you can download individual results or a ZIP package.

cta.title: Ready to Split Your Image?
cta.description: Upload your image, choose a split mode and grid settings, preview the results, then download individual files or a ZIP package.

proTips.title: How to Get Better Results with AI Image Splitter
proTips.subtitle: Practical tips to improve split quality and export efficiency

items.title: Use High-Quality Images
items.description: Use a clear, high-resolution source image whenever possible. It can significantly improve clarity and overall visual quality in each split section.

items.title: Choose the Right Aspect Ratio and Split Mode
items.description: Before splitting, choose the right aspect ratio preset and split mode based on your use case and the source image. This helps reduce repeated adjustments later.

items.title: Keep the Main Subject Intact
items.description: When adjusting the selection box, keep the main subject as complete as possible in each split result.

items.title: Preview Before Exporting
items.description: Check rows, columns, and split boundaries first. After confirming the real-time preview looks correct, export individual files or a ZIP to avoid rework.

items.title: Choose Output Format by Use Case
items.description: For social platforms, prioritize smaller file sizes (e.g., JPG). For transparency or fine detail retention, choose PNG. For web use, WebP is usually the best choice.
